'''Ben Galea''' (born 16 August 1978) is an Australian former professional [[rugby league]] footballer. A [[City New South Wales rugby league team|City New South Wales]] representative forward, he previously played in Australasia's [[National Rugby League]] for the [[Balmain Tigers]] and the [[Wests Tigers]] (with whom he won the [[2005 NRL Premiership]]) and also for English clubs [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] and [[Hull F.C.]] of the [[Super League]].

==Background==
Galea was born on 16 August 1978, in [[Parramatta]], and is of Maltese descent.<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.malteserugbyleague.com/fans |title=Fans – Malta Rugby League |accessdate=2009-09-09 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20100403015147/http://www.malteserugbyleague.com/fans |archive-date=3 April 2010 }}</ref> While attending Marayong John Paul II, he played for the [[Australia national schoolboys rugby league team|Australian Schoolboys team]] in 1996.<ref>{{cite web|title=SportingPulse Homepage for Australian Secondary Schools Rugby League|url=http://www.sportingpulse.com/assoc_page.cgi?c=7-2130-0-0-0&sID=26424|accessdate=2008-10-10|publisher=SportingPulse|archive-date=21 August 2008|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20080821104534/http://www.sportingpulse.com/assoc_page.cgi?c=7-2130-0-0-0&sID=26424|url-status=dead}}</ref>

==Playing career==
After struggling to make an impact in the lower grades at the [[Parramatta Eels]], Galea signed with the newly merged [[Wests Tigers]].

===National Rugby League===
In 2000, Galea in made a handful of NRL appearances for the Tigers from the bench. In 2001, Galea became a regular member of the Tigers' team.<ref>{{cite web|title=Ben Galea
|url=http://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/players/ben-galea/summary.html|accessdate=29 December 2010| 
publisher=Rugby League Project}}</ref> He represented City and was named the club's player of the year.<ref>{{cite journal| editor=David Middleton |title=2010 Official Rugby League Annual|year=2010|publisher=News Magazines for the [[National Rugby League]]|location=Alexandria NSW, 2015
}}</ref> After missing the first 2 games of the season, he played in every other game.

In 2003, Galea played 14 games before his season ended when he needed a shoulder reconstruction.<ref>{{cite news|author=Greg Prichard|work=Sun Herald|title=Tigers set to turn dollars into sense|url=http://newsstore.fairfax.com.au/apps/viewDocument.ac?page=1&sy=nstore&kw=brett+hodgson&pb=all_ffx&dt=selectRange&dr=entire&so=relevance&sf=text&sf=headline&rc=100&rm=200&sp=adv&clsPage=1&docID=SHD030817V8EQJ0KQ879|accessdate=6 February 2013|date=17 August 2003}}</ref>

Galea played at second-row forward in the [[2005 NRL grand final]] victory over the [[North Queensland Cowboys]]. In the moments after the grand final victory, Galea was interviewed by Channel 9 sideline commentator [[Matthew Johns]] and was asked how it felt to be a premiership winner. Galea replied, "This is unbelievable, fucking all the boys have stuck together all year, we won the comp, we're number one, fucking ..." In the following days, the Wests Tigers club issued an apology for the inappropriate language used by some of their players.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.news.com.au/sport/nrl/matty-johns-relives-the-infamous-aftermath-of-the-2005-grand-final/news-story/7e8056941f0d7c9fcd62c74d0f18a6ad/|title=Matty Johns relives the infamous aftermath of the 2005 Grand Final|publisher=News.com.au}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.smh.com.au/sport/tigers-apologise-for-bad-language-20051005-gdm6pz.html/|title=Tigers apologise for bad language|website=The Sydney Morning Herald|date=4 October 2005 }}</ref>

As NRL Premiers, Wests travelled to England to play against [[Super League]] champions [[Bradford Bulls]] in the [[2006 World Club Challenge]]. Galea played at lock forward in the Tigers' 30–10 loss.

When Galea moved from the Wests Tigers at the end of the [[2007 NRL season]], he was the joint venture's second most capped player with 150 games, and second highest try scorer, with 32 tries. In 2017, Galea was made a Wests Tiger Life Member.<ref>{{cite web| work= weststigers.com.au | title=2017 Presentation Night Awards Winners| date=5 September 2017| url=http://www.weststigers.com.au/news/2017/09/05/_2017_presentation_n.html|archive-date=6 September 2017|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170906075536/http://www.weststigers.com.au/news/2017/09/05/_2017_presentation_n.html}}</ref>

===Super League===
From 2008 to 2012, Galea played in the [[Super League]] for [[Hull Kingston Rovers]]. On 13 September 2012, Galea confirmed that he had reversed his decision to retire in order to sign a one-year deal for former West Tigers assistant [[Peter Gentle]] at [[Hull F.C.|Hull FC]].

===Highlights===
*''Junior Club:'' Penrith and Blacktown Workers
*''First Grade Debut:'' 1999 – Round 14, Balmain v [[Western Suburbs Magpies]] at [[Campbelltown Stadium]], 6 June
*''Premierships:'' Member of Wests Tigers 2005 Premiership team

==Referee==
As of the 2014 [[NRL]] season, Galea works regularly as a video referee in the Australian competition.<ref>{{cite news| work= Border Mail| title=Bulldogs coach Des Hasler urges NRL to set up video referees' bunker to help ease frustration| first = Michael| last = Carayannis| url=http://www.bordermail.com.au/story/2470883/bulldogs-coach-des-hasler-urges-nrl-to-set-up-video-referees-bunker-to-help-ease-frustration/?cs=2382|accessdate=12 August 2014|date=7 August 2014}}</ref>
